A screen pass is a type of offensive play in American football where the quarterback throws the ball to a receiver who is closely covered by an offensive lineman, usually a tight end or a running back, who blocks the defender. The screen pass is intended to give the receiver a short pass that can lead to a gain if they can break free from the block. The quarterback is supposed to throw the ball quickly after the snap, usually before the defenders can get through the screen.
